text: Financial risks include elevated leverage and liquidity constraints. Apple's debt-to-equity ratio stood at 1.54x as of Q3 2025, with total debt of $101.7B against stockholders' equity of $65.8B. Net debt after cash equivalents was $65.4B. More concerning is the current ratio of 0.87x, indicating current liabilities of $141.1B exceed current assets of $122.5B. This working capital deficit could limit financial flexibility during periods of stress. Additionally, revenue declined 1.39% YoY in Q3 2025 to $94.0B, while net income fell 5.43% YoY to $23.4B, suggesting margin pressure or volume challenges.
